title: Write table-driven valid percentage tests
scenario: Several valid boundary cases should be easy to scan. A table-driven test keeps inputs and expected values together.
instructions: Add a #[cfg(test)] module with a test that loops over valid cases for 0, 50, and 100.

hints.body: Add a #[cfg(test)] module below the implementation.
Inside it, use a cases table like [(0, 0), (50, 50), (100, 100)] and loop with for (input, expected) in cases.
One complete solution for Write table-driven valid percentage tests is shown below.

completionExplanation: Valid Percentage boundaries are now covered by one table-driven test whose input and expected output stay side by side.
